content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the dashed center line visible bending rightward, and the car appears to be on course following the curve correctly.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 82, target = (82 + 5) % 121 = 87
- Target WP87: x=0.8921, y=4.1362
- Car: x=1.2625, y=4.2832, heading=-147.58°
- dy = 4.1362 - 4.2832 = -0.1470
- dx = 0.8921 - 1.2625 = -0.3704
- bearing = atan2(-0.1470, -0.3704) → third quadrant
- angle = atan(0.1470/0.3704) = atan(0.3969) = 21.63°
- bearing = -(180° - 21.63°) = -158.37°
- delta = -158.37° - (-147.58°) = -10.79°
- |delta| ≈ 11°: waypoint is to the RIGHT, use -10° steering at 1.5 speed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Delta of -10.8° to WP87 confirms the track continues curving right, so I apply -10° steering at 1.5 speed to follow the curve smoothly without overcorrecting.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn, negative = right turn",
    "Delta under 5° means steer 0 to avoid oscillation",
    "Delta 5-15° use ±5°; 15-30° use ±10 to ±15°; 30-50° use ±15 to ±20°; >50° use ±25°",
    "Near WP80-90, track curves right — expect negative delta values requiring right steering",
    "Speed 0.75 for |delta|>50°; 1.0 for 30-50°; 1.5 for <30°",
    "Car is slightly left of center (~0.127m) — consistent with right curve approach",
    "Heading near -147° to -158° corresponds to westward/southwest travel on this section",
    "Track curves right after WP80 toward WP85-90 — negative delta values expected",
    "Zero steering when delta < 5° prevents oscillation and maintains speed",
    "Track width ~0.76m — small corrections are sufficient, avoid overcorrecting"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.506, 1.423, 1.335, 1.263],
    "y": [4.470, 4.407, 4.337, 4.283],
    "yaw": [-158.68, -153.48, -148.95, -147.58]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP87 at (0.892, 4.136): dy=-0.147, dx=-0.370, bearing=-158.37°, delta=-10.8° → waypoint to the RIGHT, steer -10°",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": -10.0
}
```
